title: Grassy Portuguese Chicken Wings Tagine
description: A comforting Portuguese dish featuring juicy chicken wings in a rich miso marinade, cooked to perfection in a ceramic tagine, with a grassy flavor profile. This low sodium friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Whisk together miso paste, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, parsley, thyme, and bay leaves in a large bowl.
Add the chicken wings to the bowl and toss to coat with the miso marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and place it in the ceramic tagine.
Pour the chicken broth into the tagine and bring to a boil on the stovetop.
Cover the tagine with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 30 minutes, then add the chopped spinach to the tagine.
Continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the spinach is wilted.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
